name: cleverreach
description: Manage CleverReach recipient groups, subscribers, and email mailings for newsletter campaigns

requiredApis.name: CleverReach OAuth app (Account > Extras > REST API)

tools.name: List Groups
tools.description: List CleverReach groups (recipient lists) so agents can find a group ID before reading or adding recipients

order.description: Order-by statement, such as 'created ASC', 'created DESC', 'changed ASC', or 'changed DESC'

tools.name: List Group Receivers
tools.description: List the receivers (recipients) of a CleverReach group, with paging and email filters

detail.description: Detail depth, bitwise combinable (0: none, 1: events, 2: orders, 4: tags)

email_list.description: Comma-separated receiver emails to filter for, such as 'foo@bar.com,bar@foo.com'

order_by.description: Field to order by, append 'asc' or 'desc' with a space

tools.name: Add Receiver
tools.description: Add (subscribe) a new receiver to a CleverReach group

groupId.description: CleverReach group (recipient list) ID to add the receiver to

registered.description: Unix timestamp of registration; provide the current time for a new receiver

activated.description: Unix timestamp of activation; set to mark the receiver as active (for example after double opt-in)

source.description: Origin identifier describing where the receiver came from

attributes.description: Group-local attribute values keyed by attribute name

global_attributes.description: Account-global attribute values keyed by attribute name, such as firstname and lastname

tags.description: Tags to attach to the receiver

tools.name: List Mailings
tools.description: List CleverReach mailings (email campaigns) filtered by state, such as drafts or finished mailings

state.description: State of the mailings to return, such as all, draft, running, or finished

limit.description: Number of mailings to return (max 1000; max 100 when state is not 'all')

channel_id.description: Only return mailings of this channel

start.description: Filter by start time (Unix timestamp; interpretation depends on state)

end.description: Filter by end time (Unix timestamp; interpretation depends on state)

page.description: Page number, zero-based; only effective when state is not 'all'

tools.name: Create Mailing Draft
tools.description: Create a new CleverReach mailing as a draft with subject, sender, and HTML/text content; sending is triggered separately

receivers.description: Receiver definition, such as { "groups": ["<groupId>"] } and optional filter

settings.description: Mailing settings, such as editor, open/link tracking, and unsubscribe options

tools.name: Get Mailing
tools.description: Get a single mailing by ID, e.g. to inspect a draft created with Create Mailing Draft

mailingId.description: Mailing ID from List Mailings or Create Mailing Draft

tools.name: Deactivate Receiver
tools.description: Deactivate (unsubscribe) a receiver in a group by ID or email — the counterpart to Add Receiver for unsubscribe handling

groupId.description: Group (list) ID the receiver belongs to

tools.name: Get Group Stats
tools.description: Get list health statistics for a group: active, inactive, and bounced receiver counts

prompts.title: Subscriber overview
prompts.prompt: List my CleverReach groups and summarize each list, then show the most recent receivers of the largest group.

prompts.title: Draft a newsletter
prompts.prompt: Create a CleverReach mailing draft for my main recipient group with a subject and HTML content I describe, and report the draft ID.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: CleverReach setup

steps.title: Create a CleverReach account
steps.description: Sign up at cleverreach.com — the free Lite plan (up to 250 recipients) is enough for development and testing.

steps.title: Create an OAuth app
steps.description: In your CleverReach account go to Account > Extras > REST API and create an OAuth app. Note the Client ID and Client Secret it contains, and register your redirect (callback) URL.

steps.title: Store the credentials and connect
steps.description: Set CLEVERREACH_CLIENT_ID and CLEVERREACH_CLIENT_SECRET, then complete the OAuth authorization-code flow: the user logs in at the CleverReach consent screen and the returned code is exchanged for an access token.

notes: The authorization request must include the extra parameter grant=basic alongside client_id, response_type=code, and redirect_uri.
Access tokens are long-lived (expires_in in the token response, for example 31536000 seconds) and a refresh_token is issued for renewing them via grant_type=refresh_token.
Each OAuth token is scoped to the CleverReach account that authorized the app; the app's permissions are configured on the OAuth app itself rather than via per-request scopes.
